name: CMB large-scale (low-l) statistical anomalies
summary: Several large-angle (low-multipole, l < 30) features of the CMB temperature map are unlikely under the LambdaCDM Gaussian-isotropic null: (a) the quadrupole-octopole alignment ('axis of evil'), (b) a hemispherical power asymmetry between northern and southern ecliptic hemispheres at ~3 sigma, (c) the Cold Spot in the southern hemisphere (~5 sigma below local mean), (d) suppressed quadrupole power and low variance on large angular scales. Individually each anomaly is 2-3 sigma; jointly they form a ~3 sigma+ statistical tension confirmed across COBE/WMAP/Planck. Either a posteriori statistical inflation, foreground systematic, or a real anisotropic component (bianchi models, non-trivial topology, pre-inflationary relic).

characteristic_numbers.note: Vielva et al. 2004 wavelet analysis; ~5 sigma local deviation. ISW from a supervoid (Granett et al. 2008) accounts for only ~10% of the dip.
Dipole modulation amplitude on scales l < 64; ~3 sigma tension with isotropy. Planck 2018 VII, arXiv:1906.02552.
C_2 and C_3 multipole vectors are highly aligned; p-value ~1-3% depending on statistic. Schwarz et al. 2004; reconfirmed in Planck.

principles_stressed.note: If real, requires a violation of LambdaCDM's statistical isotropy assumption at the largest scales - either an anisotropic inflaton (Ackerman-Carroll-Wise 2007), a coupling to a primordial Bianchi-type background, non-trivial topology (T^3 / dodecahedral) at the Hubble scale, or a pre-inflationary relic surface. None is favored by full-likelihood CMB analyses but none is excluded either. Plausibly an a posteriori multiplicity-of-tests effect; cosmic variance prevents definitive resolution within our Hubble volume.

history: Quadrupole-octopole alignment: de Oliveira-Costa, Tegmark, Zaldarriaga, Hamilton (2004), PRD 69, 063516. Cold Spot: Vielva, Martinez-Gonzalez, Barreiro, Sanz, Cayon (2004), ApJ 609, 22. Hemispherical asymmetry: Eriksen, Hansen, Banday, Gorski, Lilje (2004), ApJ 605, 14. Review: Schwarz, Starkman, Huterer, Copi (2016), 'CMB anomalies after Planck', Class. Quant. Grav. 33, 184001, arXiv:1510.07929. Planck 2018 isotropy paper: Planck Collab. (2020), A&A 641, A7, arXiv:1906.02552.

references.citation: Schwarz, Starkman, Huterer, Copi, 'CMB anomalies after Planck', Class. Quant. Grav. 33, 184001 (2016)
Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. VII. Isotropy and statistics of the CMB', A&A 641, A7 (2020)
